pi_items.source: Task setup script downloads 1C3W.pdb from rcsb.org and loads it into PyMOL.
export_result.sh explicitly writes JSON to this path.
export_result.sh checks for the existence of this image file.
export_result.sh checks for this text report.
verifier.py checks if the generated figure is at least 30000 bytes.
verifier.py uses this minimum threshold, and manual coordinate calculations confirm Lys216(NZ) to Asp85(OD1) distance is 3.79 A, fitting the range.
verifier.py uses this maximum threshold, and manual coordinate calculations confirm Lys216(NZ) to Asp85(OD1) distance is 3.79 A, fitting the range.
verifier.py uses this minimum threshold, and manual coordinate calculations confirm Lys216(NZ) to Asp212(OD1) distance is 3.74 A, fitting the range.
verifier.py uses this maximum threshold, and manual coordinate calculations confirm Lys216(NZ) to Asp212(OD1) distance is 3.74 A, fitting the range.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real PDB data for bacteriorhodopsin (1C3W). Independent verification confirms that the shortest distances from the Schiff base nitrogen (Lys216 NZ) to the closest side-chain oxygen atoms of Asp85 and Asp212 are ~3.79 A and ~3.74 A, respectively. These values fall perfectly within the verifier's expected tolerance ranges (3.5-4.8 A and 3.0-4.5 A). The target paths for the generated structural report and publication figure exactly match the verifier logic.
